title: Test Case Template
content: Test case fields: Test Case ID; Test Suite; Title; Preconditions (system state before test); Test Steps (numbered actions); Test Data (inputs); Expected Result (specific/measurable); Actual Result (filled during execution); Status (Pass/Fail/Blocked/Not Run); Priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low); Tester; Date executed.

title: Test Scenario vs Test Case Distinction
content: Test scenario = high-level description of what to test (User can log in with valid credentials). Test case = detailed step-by-step with specific data (Step 1: Enter username admin@test.com; Step 2: Enter password; Expected: dashboard displayed with welcome message). One scenario typically spawns 3-10 test cases.

title: UAT Planning Checklist
content: UAT planning checklist: acceptance criteria finalized and signed off; test environment provisioned and stable; test data prepared (realistic/anonymized); UAT participants identified and available; UAT schedule agreed; test cases reviewed by business; defect logging process agreed; entry criteria defined; exit criteria defined; sign-off process agreed.

title: UAT Execution Procedure
content: UAT execution steps: verify entry criteria met (environment stable/data ready); brief participants on scope and process; execute test cases in priority order; log defects immediately using agreed template; daily defect triage meeting; retest fixed defects; track metrics (pass rate/defect count/open blockers); evaluate exit criteria; escalate blockers.
tags: uat execution procedure defect triage metrics

title: Acceptance Criteria to Test Case Conversion
content: Conversion pattern: Given [precondition] → Preconditions section. When [action] → Test Steps section. Then [expected outcome] → Expected Result section. Each Given-When-Then becomes one test case. Multiple Then clauses = multiple test cases. Negative scenarios: add test case for each invalid input or boundary.

title: Boundary Value Analysis (BVA)
content: BVA tests values at boundaries of valid ranges. For range 1-100: test -1 (below min); 0 (at invalid); 1 (at min valid); 2 (just above min); 50 (middle); 99 (just below max); 100 (at max valid); 101 (just above max). Catches off-by-one errors. Essential for: age/quantity/amount/date fields.

title: Equivalence Partitioning (EP)
content: EP divides inputs into partitions where all values behave the same. One test per partition is sufficient. Example for age 18-65: invalid partition <18 (test: 17); valid partition 18-65 (test: 40); invalid partition >65 (test: 66). Combine with BVA for thorough coverage. Reduces redundant test cases.

title: Decision Table Testing
content: Decision table test: each column in decision table = one test case. Set conditions to column values; execute process; verify all specified actions occur and non-specified actions do NOT occur. Ensures complete coverage of business rule combinations. Test unspecified combinations for defensive behavior.

title: State Transition Testing
content: State transition test: identify all valid and invalid transitions; create test for each valid transition (verify new state); create test for each invalid transition (verify rejected with error); test boundary states (initial state/terminal state/error state). Maps to state machine diagram in requirements.

title: Exploratory Testing for BA
content: BA exploratory testing: time-boxed sessions (60-90 min); charter defines scope and goals; simultaneous learning/designing/executing; use mind map to track coverage; note anomalies even if not a clear defect; session debrief documents findings. Effective for discovering issues that scripted tests miss.

title: Performance Test Requirements Template
content: Performance test requirements: Load test (N concurrent users; p95 response time target; error rate <X%); Stress test (ramp up to 2x expected peak; identify breaking point); Spike test (sudden 3x load increase; recovery time <30s); Endurance test (sustained load for 4h; no memory leak/degradation). Tools: JMeter/k6/Gatling.
tags: performance-testing load stress spike requirements nfr

title: Security Test Requirements Template
content: Security test requirements: Authentication testing (brute force/session fixation/token validation); Authorization testing (privilege escalation/IDOR); Input validation (SQL injection/XSS/command injection); HTTPS configuration (TLS version/cipher suites); Secrets detection (no API keys in code/logs); Dependency vulnerability scan (OWASP Dependency-Check).

title: API Test Requirements Template
content: API test requirements: Happy path for each endpoint (valid input → expected response); Validation tests (invalid input → 400 with error details); Authentication tests (no token/expired token/wrong scope → 401/403); Rate limit tests (exceed limit → 429 with retry-after); Contract tests (response schema matches OpenAPI spec).

title: Regression Test Requirements
content: Regression test requirements: define regression suite from critical path test cases; automate regression (target 80% automation coverage); run on every build (CI/CD pipeline); full regression before each release; regression scope expanded when defects found outside suite; test execution time target (<30 min for core regression).

title: Test Data Requirements
content: Test data requirements: realistic data matching production volumes; anonymized/synthetic (no real PII in test environments); data setup/teardown automated; test data versioned in source control; dedicated datasets per test type (smoke/regression/performance); refresh schedule for test data; edge case data explicitly created (empty sets/max size/special chars).

title: Test Environment Requirements
content: Test environment requirements: environment parity with production (same OS/versions/config); dedicated environments (dev/SIT/UAT/staging); environment provisioning automated (IaC); data refresh process documented; access control (testers have appropriate access); monitoring enabled; environment availability SLA (99% during business hours).

title: Defect Lifecycle States
content: Defect lifecycle: New (logged by tester); Open (triaged and assigned to developer); In Progress (developer working); Fixed (developer marks resolved with fix description); Ready for Retest (deployed to test environment); Verified (tester confirms fix); Closed (complete). Rejected (not a defect) and Deferred (fix in future release) are valid transitions.
tags: defect lifecycle states bug workflow triage

title: Bug Report Template
content: Bug report fields: Bug ID; Title (concise description); Severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low); Priority (P1/P2/P3/P4); Environment (OS/browser/version); Preconditions; Steps to reproduce (numbered); Expected result; Actual result; Screenshot/video attached; Related requirement/test case ID; Reporter; Date.
tags: bug-report template severity priority reproduce defect

title: Defect Severity vs Priority Distinction
content: Severity = technical impact (Critical: system crash/data loss; High: major feature broken; Medium: feature partially works; Low: cosmetic). Priority = business urgency (P1: fix immediately; P2: fix in current sprint; P3: fix in next sprint; P4: fix when convenient). High priority + low severity: typo on homepage. Low priority + high severity: crash in rarely used feature.

title: Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM)
content: RTM columns: Requirement ID; Requirement description; Test Case IDs that cover it; Test execution status; Defect IDs linked; Coverage status (covered/not covered/partially covered). Use to identify untested requirements and requirements with failing tests. Updated continuously during test execution.

title: UAT Sign-off Template
content: UAT sign-off document: Project and release name; UAT period (start/end dates); UAT scope (features tested); Test execution summary (total cases/passed/failed/blocked); Open defects list (with severity and disposition); Deferred defects (accepted risks with owner); Business sign-off statement; Authorized signatures (business owner/UAT lead/project manager); Date.

title: Smoke Test Checklist
content: Smoke test checklist (run after each deployment): application starts without errors; login works for each role; main navigation accessible; key read operations work (list/detail pages); key write operations work (create/update); critical integrations responding; error pages display correctly (404/500); no obvious UI breakage.

title: End-to-End Test Scenario Template
content: E2E scenario template: Scenario ID and name; Business process being tested; Persona (role/user type); Preconditions (system state/test data required); Steps (cross-system flow with expected state at each step); Integrations exercised; Data verified at each system boundary; Success criteria; Cleanup steps.

title: UAT Entry and Exit Criteria
content: UAT Entry criteria: SIT completed with pass rate >95%; all critical/high defects from SIT resolved; test environment stable (no deployments during UAT); test data prepared; UAT test cases reviewed and approved; participants briefed. Exit criteria: pass rate >98%; no open Critical/High defects; all deferred defects accepted by business; sign-off obtained.

title: Positive and Negative Test Case Pattern
content: For every requirement write: Positive test (valid input → expected success outcome); Negative tests — invalid input types; out-of-range values; missing required fields; duplicate data; unauthorized access attempt; concurrent modification. Ratio: 1 positive to 3-5 negative test cases recommended for critical features.

title: Test Summary Report Template
content: Test summary report: Sprint/Release ID; Test period; Scope tested; Total test cases (planned/executed/passed/failed/blocked/not run); Pass rate and trend; Defect summary (found/fixed/open by severity); Test coverage percentage (RTM); Key risks and observations; Recommendation (go/no-go); Sign-off.

title: Usability Testing Protocol for BA
content: BA usability testing: recruit 5-8 participants matching persona; prepare realistic task scenarios (not how-to instructions); use think-aloud protocol; observe without intervening; capture: task completion rate/time/errors/satisfaction; post-task SUS questionnaire; debrief for additional insights; synthesize findings into requirement updates.

title: Defect Triage Process
content: Daily defect triage: review all New defects; assign severity and priority collaboratively (BA/dev/test); assign to developer with target fix sprint; identify root cause category (requirements ambiguity/missing requirement/implementation error/environment issue); update defect metrics dashboard; escalate critical blockers immediately.
tags: defect-triage process daily severity priority assign

title: Test Case Quality Review Checklist
content: Test case quality checklist: each test case has single expected result; steps are clear and unambiguous; test data specified (not just described); preconditions fully defined; test case traceable to requirement; positive and negative scenarios both covered; no duplicate test cases; boundary values included for data validation.

title: Non-Functional Test Requirements Pattern
content: NFR test requirements template: NFR-ID reference; Metric to measure; Target value with unit; Measurement method (tool/technique); Test conditions (load profile/data volume/environment); Frequency of testing (per release/quarterly); Pass/fail threshold; Action if threshold not met.
